title: Murder accused Bevan van Druten bail hearing delayed due to lack of legal representation
bodyText:
GARDEN ROUTE NEWS - No bail application was heard today in the case of Garden Route artist Bevan van Druten (53), who stands accused of murdering his girlfriend, Nicky van Heerden (54), a week ago, due to the absence of legal representation.


> Van Heerden’s body was discovered at Rivertides near Keurbooms Beach on Sunday night, 18 January, after the police had responded to a report of a woman screaming.


Van Druten briefly appeared in the Plettenberg Bay Magistrate’s Court at about 12:45 on 26 January to be afforded the opportunity to secure legal representation. He was dressed in sheepskin slippers, a sleeveless T-shirt and baggy turquoise trousers.

A large group of people, mostly women, gathered at the court to protest against GBV and to oppose bail.

The State informed the court that Carl Jeppe, a prominent Garden Route attorney, whom Van Druten initially requested to represent him, had declined the brief. Van Druten then requested representation from a State-appointed attorney, making the request in Afrikaans.

Both the prosecutor and the state attorney subsequently asked the court for time to prepare.

During the proceedings, the State informed the court that Van Druten had been referred to the district surgeon, who recommended that he be sent for observation. Magistrate Zukiswa Hans questioned the referral, noting that no court order had been issued authorising the district surgeon to make such a recommendation.


> The court instructed the prosecutor to establish the reasons for the referral in the absence of a court order. Following this, the State withdrew its request.


The matter was postponed to this Thursday, 29 January, when Van Druten is expected to appear for a bail application.

Media outside the court this morning.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
